e: "kočka" is a Czech word that translates to "cat". Example: Černá kočka.

Hedvábí is Czech for silk, a luxurious fabric. Example: 'Její šaty jsou vyrobené z hedvábí' (Her dress is made of silk).

q: What does 'vázanka' refer to in Czech?
e: Vázanka is a Czech word for necktie or neck band. Example: 'On nosí hedvábnou vázanku' (He wears a silk necktie).

q: Which of these is a meaning of 'kůže'?
e: Kůže in Czech means both leather and skin. Examples: 'Já mám koženou peněženku' (I have a leather wallet) and 'Má jemnou kůži' (She has soft skin).

q: What does 'pásek' translate to in English?
e: Pásek is Czech for belt, a garment worn around the waist. Example: 'Můj otec nosí kožený pásek' (My father wears a leather belt).

q: What is the English translation of 'rukavice'?
e: Rukavice is Czech for glove, worn on the hand. Example: 'Tyto rukavice jsou vyrobeny z kůže' (These gloves are made of leather).
Ponožky is Czech for socks, a foot covering. Example: 'Dívka si obleče a svleče ponožky sama' (The girl puts on and takes off socks by herself).

q: What is the English word for 'boty'?
e: Boty is Czech for shoes. Example: 'Ona má mnoho párů bot' (She has many pairs of shoes).
Tenisky is Czech for sneakers or athletic shoes. Example: 'On si vyzul tenisky' (He took off his sneakers).

q: What is the English translation of 'sandály'?
e: Sandály is Czech for sandals. Example: 'On nosí sandály na pláž' (He wears sandals to the beach).
Vysoké podpatky means high heels in Czech. Example: 'Ona nosí červené vysoké podpatky' (She wears red high heels).

s: The Czech phrase 'Obchody s obuví' means:
q: What is the English translation of 'Obchody s obuví'?
e: Obchody s obuví means shoe store in Czech. Example: 'Zde jsou obchody s obuví' (There are shoe stores here).
Prodat is Czech for to sell. Example: 'On prodal své tenisky' (He sold his sneakers).

q: What is the English translation of 'kožené boty'?
e: Kožené boty means leather shoes in Czech. Example: 'Tento obchod s obuví prodává kožené boty' (This shoe store sells leather shoes).
Oprava bot means shoe repair store in Czech. Example: 'Zde je opravna obuvi' (Here is a shoe repair shop).

q: What is the English translation of 'opravit'?
e: Opravit means to repair or to fix in Czech. Example: 'Já jsem opravil ten stůl sám' (I repaired the table myself).

q: What does 'podrážka boty' refer to in English?
e: Podrážka boty means the sole of a shoe in Czech. Example: 'Chci opravit podrážku na botě' (I want to repair the sole of the shoe).

q: What does 'guma' translate to in English?
e: Guma means rubber in Czech. Example: 'Podrážka na botě je z gumy' (The sole of the shoe is made of rubber).

q: What is the English translation of 'tkanička'?
e: Tkanička means shoelace in Czech. Example: 'Měl bych si zavázat tkaničku' (I should tie my shoelace).
Sportovní obuv means sports shoes in Czech. Example: 'Sem by jsi měl nosit sportovní obuv' (You should wear sports shoes here).

q: What does 'volný' translate to in English?
e: Volný means loose in Czech. Example: 'Tento pár bot s vysokými podpatky je volný' (This pair of high heel shoes is loose).

q: What is the English translation of 'těsný'?
e: Těsný means tight in Czech. Example: 'Tento pár tenisek je těsný' (This pair of sneakers is tight).

q: Which of these is a meaning of 'noha'?
e: Noha in Czech means both foot and leg. Examples: 'Její noha je velmi malá' (Her foot is very small) and 'Jeho nohy jsou delší než moje nohy' (His legs are longer than my legs).
Pondělí is Czech for Monday. Example: 'Já plavu každé pondělí' (I swim every Monday).

q: What is the English translation of 'sobota'?
e: Sobota is Czech for Saturday. Example: 'Při sobotách koukám na filmy se svým otcem' (On Saturdays I watch movies with my father).
Víkend is Czech for weekend. Example: 'My se učíme zpívat každý víkend' (We learn to sing every weekend).

q: What is the English translation of 'večer'?
e: Večer is Czech for evening. Example: 'On jí každý večer s přáteli' (He eats every evening with friends).

q: Which of these is a meaning of 'měsíc'?
e: Měsíc in Czech means both month and moon. Examples: 'Ona chodí nakupovat do obchodu každý měsíc' (She shops at the store every month) and 'Měsíc je menší než Země' (The moon is smaller than Earth).
Každý den means every day in Czech. Example: 'Já chodím do školy každý den' (I go to school every day).

q: What is the English translation of 'čtvrtek'?
e: Čtvrtek is Czech for Thursday. Example: 'Ve čtvrtek se obvykle učím čínsky' (On Thursday I usually study Chinese).
Neděle is Czech for Sunday. Example: 'V neděli obvykle chodím do kavárny' (On Sunday I usually go to a café).

q: What is the English translation of 'odpoledne'?
e: Odpoledne is Czech for afternoon. Example: 'On obvykle hraje fotbal v neděli odpoledne' (He usually plays soccer on Sunday afternoon).
Ráno is Czech for morning. Example: 'Ona pije obvykle kávu ráno' (She usually drinks coffee in the morning).

q: What is the English translation of 'schůzka'?
e: Schůzka means to hang out or a meeting in Czech. Examples: 'V neděli odpoledne obvykle mívám schůzku se svými přáteli' (On Sunday afternoon I usually have a meeting with my friends) and 'Začneme schůzku' (Let's start the meeting).
Dnes is Czech for today. Example: 'Dnes je pondělí' (Today is Monday).

q: What is the English translation of 'všední den'?
e: Všední den means weekday in Czech. Example: 'Pondělí je všední den' (Monday is a weekday).
Svátek is Czech for holiday. Example: 'Dnes je svátek' (Today is a holiday).

q: What is the English translation of 'leden'?
e: Leden is Czech for January. Example: 'Leden má 31 dnů' (January has 31 days).
Příští měsíc means next month in Czech. Example: 'Příští měsíc bude 5 svátků' (Next month there will be 5 holidays).

q: What is the English translation of 'zítra'?
e: Zítra is Czech for tomorrow. Example: 'Zítra je pondělí' (Tomorrow is Monday).
Nový rok means New Year's Day in Czech. Example: 'Dnes je Nový rok' (Today is New Year's Day).

q: What is the English translation of '1. ledna'?
e: 1. ledna means January first in Czech. Example: 'Den Nového roku je prvního ledna' (New Year's Day is on January first).

s: The Czech phrase 'den svatého Valentýna' means:
q: What does 'den svatého Valentýna' refer to?
e: Den svatého Valentýna means Valentine's Day in Czech. Example: 'Zítra je den svatého Valentýna' (Tomorrow is Valentine's Day).

q: What is the English translation of '14. února'?
e: 14. února means February fourteenth in Czech. Example: 'Den svatého Valentýna je 14.února' (Valentine's Day is February 14th).
Vánoce is Czech for Christmas. Example: 'Vánoce jsou svátek' (Christmas is a holiday).

q: What is the English translation of '25. prosince'?
e: 25. prosince means December twenty-fifth in Czech. Example: 'Vánoce jsou 25.prosince' (Christmas is December 25th).
Sedm ráno means 7 am in Czech. Example: 'Je sedm dopoledne' (It is 7 am).

s: The Czech phrase 'tři čtvrtě na ...' means:
q: What is the English translation of 'tři čtvrtě na ...'?
e: Tři čtvrtě na ... means quarter to in Czech. Example: 'Je třičtvrtě na 7 ráno' (It is quarter to 7 in the morning).

q: What does '... půl' refer to in Czech time?
e: Půl means half in Czech time expressions. Example: 'Ona jde do obchodu v půl osmé' (She goes to the shop at 7:30).

q: What is the English translation of 'čtvrt na ...'?
e: Čtvrt na ... means quarter past in Czech. Example: 'Jdu do školy ve čtvrt na osm' (I go to school at quarter past eight).

q: What does 'nyní' translate to in English?
e: Nyní is Czech for now. Example: 'Teď je přesně 10 hodin' (It is exactly 10 o'clock now).
